reasoning: Currently, the 2025 domestic box office is already being led by A Minecraft Movie, which has grossed over $421 million as of late May/early June. Current projections for The Fantastic Four: First Steps (releasing in late July) suggest an opening weekend around $125M-$136M and a domestic total around $335M-$350M. This implies Fantastic Four has heavily constrained upside and is unlikely to even beat the current $423M ceiling set by Minecraft. Additionally, Fantastic Four has to contend with an overcrowded 2025 blockbuster slate including Jurassic World 4, Superman, Zootopia 2, and Avatar 3. Since Fantastic Four would need extreme, unprecedented overperformance relative to tracking just to beat a film that has already banked $423M—and then would still have to survive against all aforementioned heavyweights—its chances of finishing #1 in 2025 Calendar Gross are minuscule. The Polymarket consensus of 2% in late May appropriately reflects this nearly insurmountable path to #1.
